assumptions: The question is about hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 for foreign exchange risk arising from an intragroup dividend receivable.
The analysis is limited to identifying IFRS hedge accounting models potentially applicable at a top level, not to concluding eligibility on the specific facts.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge accounting for recognised assets and liabilities as a top-level hedge accounting model, which can be relevant when the hedged item is a recognised receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge accounting for forecast transactions and cash flow variability as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model, including intragroup foreign currency exposures in limited cases in consolidated accounts.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedging of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate top-level hedge accounting model in consolidated financial statements.
